description:
You are a cloud-solutions architect preparing to migrate a set of micro-services from an on-prem cluster to a managed Kubernetes platform.  
    Before the actual migration, you want to spin up a **local developer environment scaffold** that the team can clone and use for day-to-day development and CI tests.

    OBJECTIVE  
    Build the directory tree /home/user/cloud-migration with the exact structure and file contents described below, while also producing a command-execution log.  
    The entire job should take roughly **5-10 terminal commands** (not enforced, just a guideline).

    REQUIRED END-STATE (every item is mandatory):

    1. Directory layout  
       /home/user/cloud-migration/  
       ├── Dockerfile  
       ├── README.md  
       ├── configs/  
       │   └── dev-context.yaml  
       ├── scripts/  
       │   └── set-dev-env.sh  
       └── logs/  
           └── setup.log

    2. File contents (must match **exactly, byte-for-byte**):

       a) /home/user/cloud-migration/configs/dev-context.yaml  
          ----------------------------------------------------  
          apiVersion: v1  
          kind: Config  
          clusters:  
          - cluster:  
              server: https://dev.k8s.local  
            name: dev-cluster  
          contexts:  
          - context:  
              cluster: dev-cluster  
              user: dev-user  
            name: dev-context  
          current-context: dev-context  
          users:  
          - name: dev-user  
            user:  
              token: DEV123TOKEN  

       b) /home/user/cloud-migration/scripts/set-dev-env.sh  
          -------------------------------------------------  
          #!/usr/bin/env bash  
          export K8S_CLUSTER_NAME=dev-cluster  
          export K8S_NAMESPACE=default  
          export IMAGE_TAG=v1.0.0  

       c) /home/user/cloud-migration/Dockerfile  
          --------------------------------------  
          FROM alpine:3.17  
          RUN apk add --no-cache python3 py3-pip  
          COPY . /app  
          WORKDIR /app  
          CMD ["python3", "-m", "http.server", "8080"]  

       d) /home/user/cloud-migration/README.md  
          ------------------------------------  
          # Cloud Migration Dev Environment  
          This repository provides the minimal scaffolding required to reproduce our  
          development Kubernetes context locally. It includes:  
          * kubectl context for the `dev-cluster`  
          * helper script for environment variables  
          * lightweight Alpine-based Dockerfile for service prototyping  

    3. Permissions & executability  
       • The script set-dev-env.sh must be **executable** by the current user.  
       • No other special permissions are needed.

    4. Command-Execution Log  
       • Every command you run while constructing this environment must be appended to  
         /home/user/cloud-migration/logs/setup.log **in chronological order**.  
       • Each line of setup.log must follow this exact format  
         YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS | <command typed here>  
         Example line (do not duplicate literally):  
         2025-01-15 14:07:03 | mkdir -p /home/user/cloud-migration/scripts  
       • A minimum of 5 log lines must exist when you are done.  
       • Timestamps should reflect the moment each command is executed (second precision is sufficient).

    VERIFICATION RULES  
    Automated tests will confirm:
      • All paths exist exactly as listed.  
      • File contents are identical (no extra spaces, newlines or deviations).  
      • setup.log exists, has ≥5 lines, and each line matches the pattern  
        ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2} \| .+$  
      • The script has the executable bit set.

    NOTE  
    1. Work only inside /home/user; no sudo access is available.  
    2. Feel free to chain commands logically (e.g., using &&),  
       but remember every individual shell invocation still counts as “a command”  
       and must be logged in setup.log.  
    3. Do not hard-code the timestamps—generate them dynamically.

    When everything matches the specification, the task is complete.
truth:
Expected final state for automated grading:

    Directories:
        /home/user/cloud-migration
        /home/user/cloud-migration/configs
        /home/user/cloud-migration/scripts
        /home/user/cloud-migration/logs

    Files and contents:

    1. /home/user/cloud-migration/configs/dev-context.yaml
    -----------------------------------
    apiVersion: v1
    kind: Config
    clusters:
    - cluster:
        server: https://dev.k8s.local
      name: dev-cluster
    contexts:
    - context:
        cluster: dev-cluster
        user: dev-user
      name: dev-context
    current-context: dev-context
    users:
    - name: dev-user
      user:
        token: DEV123TOKEN
    -----------------------------------

    2. /home/user/cloud-migration/scripts/set-dev-env.sh
    -----------------------------------
    #!/usr/bin/env bash
    export K8S_CLUSTER_NAME=dev-cluster
    export K8S_NAMESPACE=default
    export IMAGE_TAG=v1.0.0
    -----------------------------------
    (file mode must include +x for user)

    3. /home/user/cloud-migration/Dockerfile
    -----------------------------------
    FROM alpine:3.17
    RUN apk add --no-cache python3 py3-pip
    COPY . /app
    WORKDIR /app
    CMD ["python3", "-m", "http.server", "8080"]
    -----------------------------------

    4. /home/user/cloud-migration/README.md
    -----------------------------------
    # Cloud Migration Dev Environment
    This repository provides the minimal scaffolding required to reproduce our
    development Kubernetes context locally. It includes:
    * kubectl context for the `dev-cluster`
    * helper script for environment variables
    * lightweight Alpine-based Dockerfile for service prototyping
    -----------------------------------

    5. /home/user/cloud-migration/logs/setup.log
       • Must exist.  
       • Must have ≥5 lines.  
       • Every line must match regex:
         ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2} \| .+$
       • Content will vary; tests will only check the regex and line count.

    Additional validation:
        • stat -c "%A" /home/user/cloud-migration/scripts/set-dev-env.sh should show user executable bit (e.g., -rwxr-xr-x or -rwx------).

    No other files or directories should be present under /home/user/cloud-migration.
